channel.title: On Writing Love
channel.description: This is where i post what i write from Novels, Peotry, Lyrics, Journals and More. Anything That Has A Purpose Or Interest Me.

item.title: Acceptance
item.description:
Jezz my bad, all this time you've proved me wrong
                                    how so?
Welcome to the other side of road, Look up in the mirror 
          watch the sadness and pain
Nothing matters than messing up more than once
Cause' feels like throwing up all together again
Forgot we ain't patient enough (Oh I need time)
Shit still hunts me its hard to fake how i feel
Every night i go out to get high, just for my memories to 
            make it worse
My body fighting to survive but my mind
            just wanna fucking die (wanna die)
I know how its hard to fake how you feel (I can see through)
Everywhere i go i see a blurry face in my memory (wasted)
My words might do nothing, but thats how i smoke my feelings
Its like smoking a blunt but letting it go away everytime
Inside my mind, still tryna make things work. Guess 
                    we all mentally ill (no shame about it)
I can hear the silence on the other side, just the side
Cause' It's not the end of the road
            Oh Feel it, ooh feel it, feel it (again)
These memories, they won't get the best of me .
            So light up a joint for me

View/Download This Lyrics From Here

item.title: Unflattered
item.description:
Look at me and watch, whats been redoubt
Takes my strength and my emotions away and see
I'm still delighting far away, ain't falling caus' ya
I forgot the convo days and phony nights
Splash of time blaze to doom
I've lost grip and turned into skyline
Make directions make it easy, unforgiving
Never mind, we're already doomed too soon
I've lost touch, i've said too much, been opposites and such. 
                                But i'lllll (what?)

Forget about dwelling what "could've" been
I saw you wasting time, tik-tok out of your mind
I don't blame ya for being unconscious all the time
I've seen enough, i've had too much, been unflattered and such. 
                                But I wish it waas alll ...
From my mind, from my mind, from my mind
From my mind, from my mind, from my mind
From my mind, from my mind, from my mind.

Save/Download This Lyrics From Here

item.title: Slow Me
item.description:
I can't stand going fast at the times
My heartbeat rings like bell, silent and dim
I always thought i could make it on my own
\vin For forgetting embracin' thru
Wandering the lands looking for words to unmake it right
Chances flying, keep smackin' them all the time
Tell me something i don't know. Why can't u say
I know that's why you mind can't say, Ya know
\vin Lo'e, is like a sinking stone
\vin \vin Drop it twice and it's begone
The story is different, i can feel it knocking
The story is different, bounce of lightening striking
The earth is spinning, with us time is moving
Slow me, Slow me, Slow me, Slow me
I'm moving way too fast, i'm getting away
Stop me thru, Slow me down, Losing myself
Its starting again, freshness of life glacing away
You Turning away from me, from my mind
Sometimes you can't be new but it's worth a try.

View/Download This Poem From Here

item.title: Last Chapter
item.description:
Who thought it could've been like this for all i wished gone
Who thought it could've changed into a stranger's look away
I remember, I remember seeing you for the first time.
I remember walking up puzzeled trying match perfect things
I remember last time the happiness and pain.
I remember whats all left just memories (now memories)
So why can't i lose all the momories now, why can't i
So much sacrificed for left by unwakened dream
I'm trapped in this sordid game, where the ending's
                                          All the same


I tried to heal your heart with all that i could
                              But got mine broken instead
Ever since the day you left my fate been set alone
Tell me how many years to walk this path alone? (again)
So much we planned ahead, so why'd you choose to leave
                              Why cant i?
Nothing is harder than to wake up all alone, Realize
    it's not okay, it's the end of all we known
Time keeps passing by but its seems frozen still
Tell me do you still even feel a thing?
Scars are left behind but some too deep to feel
And i've lost my power to feel anymore
Gave you all i had to give, i hope its worth it

When all is gone and can't be regained
    We can't seem to shelter the pain inside
The pain is strong and urges rise, your pain is gone
Time shows now I think I understand my mistake
Like a memoir i saw it through, In the end
                        I'd give my life for you.
Once i stood so tall now i'm searching for a sign
Nothing lasts forever for all good times we had
    I'd choose to trade it all to bring you back
Some day, some-day when you'll open your mind
You'll find a mistake that dragged us all behind
Non' can't be real, u took a piece of me with you.
Cause' If you'd only see the picture's clear as today, 
                        you'd still be around

Oooh I can hear the voice's wanting you to stay, 
    I suppress them voice's cause' ain't gonna be the same 
They say all love can never die, I say it just moves on
Oh, i don't need your regret apologizes. Save it for another time
Cause we all just need a reason, a reason to stay
But some can't be bothered to stay for another day
Oh I ain't worried about anything no more
Cause' there's somebody that will love us all
But if we can't be in no relationship with this person and 
    make it work
How you gonn' go be in another relationship and 
    make it work, it's the same thing


It's the end of the road, the last chapter remains
Found a place for you in my mind, where you can stay away
Everything can finally fade, I hope you find your own way
What's left to find, the big prize wasted and the days are gone.

                        Thank you, Good-bye

View/Download This Lyrics From Here

item.title: Ana Jay
item.description:
Wa chof fin wslat
B9it kantsana o kantsana
chkon kan kaygol, tri9 fin ghadi twaslna
Klit d9a f9albi, ob9it sakt
dawiti 3liya dnya okhalitini kanbghik
9lila fha9ek had stora
Masm3tch lharda
kola chhal hadi nsit o khwit
No,no Bzaff tlhob lik
Hbiba, 9albk dima kaysoni
Jamais n3awdo nafs lghalat
We chof dnya chhal sghira
9albi chwiya o tfara
lbarh bohdi O lyoum hdaya
F 3ini kanchofk wf 9albi 9albk
love 3amri antal9k mazal
No, no bzaff
    Mab9a walo
            For you

Save/Download This Poem From Here

item.title: I Wanna
item.description:
When i hear your voice
I'd look at the roof
   And think of everyway
   I'd see you soon
If you'd look at the sky
   You'd know..
   I ain't far away
I wanna get you more, often
   To see your joy
   To take you on up
   To hold you under
   To kiss you undone
I'd never seek a better
       Being your lover
I've got a feeling for you
       Nothing else matter
              For you

Save/Download This Poem From Here

item.title: Give Me A Sign
item.description:
I can't hold myself anymore.
Hopelessly waiting for you to come.
Thinking of the times that we have.
I keep my mind for you, to stay.
Holding back, hoping for you to ask.

I have see myself turning into dark
    Losing my existence everyday.
    Don't wanna see myself replacing you.
I know that you are afraid.
    So just let me show you.
    There is no one in the world than you
    Who has won my heart.
I will forgiving you for hurting myself. As
    Sometimes thru all of the things
    We think and say love can be a breath away

I lost you once but don't want to lose you twice.
        Will you ever make a sign
                    For you

Save/Download This Poem From Here

item.title: Epilogue
item.description:
* The Rebirth…*

Two words as nonthreatening as words come. But put them together side-by-side and they have the power to haunt you for the rest of your life:…

Sixteen days went by since you’ve made the decision of leaving her. Yet, you still haven’t found the courage to write the exact words — or perhaps those that you remember — that were said during that afternoon. Likely because, once again, even if every fibre of your being knew that that was the only possible path, you kept hope, as always, that the world would show you things could be different. And, in that last breath of hope, you’ve let — you’ve forced — her to be the one saying out loud what both knew to be the purpose of that meeting, that day, that conversation, that last embrace. There’s no place for pride in the game of love, and the only way for you to approach the certainty you were looking for was, once again, to put in her hands the decision of both of your fates.

And ever since, those words haunt your days, your dreams, your unconscious mind. You recurrently ponder every hypothesis, every past decision where things could have been different. Perhaps if — at that time, at that point, when she said what she have said — you’ve reacted differently. If at least you’ve tried to be more understanding, more tolerant, less worried, more disconnected… If you’ve had the courage, or the patience, of giving time to time…

That hateful sentence… That concept that seems to be against everything that defines you, that defines what you’ve learned to be in these past years, so against the human condition. Time, our worst enemy, simultaneously the source of our existence — no one exists as an immutable being — and our doom:

We live on the future: “tomorrow”, “later on”, “when you have made your way”, “you will understand when you are old enough”. Such irrelevancies are wonderful, for, after all, it’s a matter of dying. Yet a day comes when a man notices or says that he is thirty. Thus he asserts his youth. But simultaneously he situates himself in relation to time. He takes his place in it. He admits that he stands at a certain point on a curve that he acknowledges having to travel to its end. He belongs to time, and by the horror that seizes him, he recognizes his worst enemy. Tomorrow, he was longing for tomorrow, whereas everything in him ought to reject it. - Albert Camus

And yet, without time, there is no hope, for:

A man devoid of hope and conscious of being so has ceased to belong to the future. - Albert Camus

And from this realization, deliberately or not, your mind decided to do what it does best every single day: to analyze the situation, to ponder the alternatives, to create solutions, to test hypothesis; if the solution doesn’t work, if the hypothesis doesn’t hold, then one has to simply backtrack, to ponder where it went wrong, to look for the detail that eluded us; because something eluded us… something always eludes us:

And if it really becomes necessary, then you scratch everything, you throw all away and start from the beginning! Yet another course of action that comes so easy for you; something that would scare so many people or be simply regarded as a complete waste. But not to you; you never hesitate in throwing away hundreds of hours of work. Why? Because nothing is lost: nothing is ever lost! You always keep something… the experience of failing, the knowledge of what doesn’t work, the intuition that leads you to join point A, with point B, with point C… but that missed something along the path; the frustration that keeps you moving, and the tenacity that there is no problem that cannot be solved… because the solution always existed — somewhere — and if it is currently out of your reach, it’s because you, and only you, don’t possess the skill to find it. The responsibility always felt down over the one and same person, and you’ve learned — or today you choose — that there’s no gain in sharing it with others. You are, simultaneously, the victim, judge, and executioner of your own failures.

But life isn’t made of that stuff; because would you think carefully, would you observe yourself writing down this text, would you contemplate the long sequence of actions, the chain of events that lead to the existence of tens of thousands of words dedicated to someone you hardly understood — someone that was probably nothing more that the idealization of your own mind — you would see that control is a mere illusion, and any attempt to comprehend the incomprehensible, the neverending quest for the meaning of life and the sensations that make us happy, are themselves the ultimate acts that impede us from being so:

What is then left to us, mere humans, also incapable of controlling that arrow of time?…

Choice…

The choice, free of every and any illusion of consequence and meaning. We are not able to control the world or the people that surround us. We cannot bend reality to our own will. But we are — we believe to be? — able to choose.

And that now depart from each other, due to the action of the exact same force that once attracted you… perhaps still looking onto one another, taking with you whatever both choose to take. While one of you, unable to forget all the wonders that were seen and felt in such a different, such fascinating, such strange but simultaneously such familiar world, forever revolted by the unshakeable laws of nature, ponder:

* The Rebirth…*
